  • Parsing
  • Resolution
  • Export

  • Orchestration
  • Executors

  • Connectors

Deployment

  • Workers
  • Integrations

Privacy & Compliance

  • Privacy
Back to top
View this page

Models¶

Core data models for the Griot contract system.

Overview¶

The models module provides dataclasses representing the core entities:

  • Schema: First-class data assets (tables, views, files)

  • Contract: Data contracts with inheritance support

  • Property: Column definitions with constraints

  • Check: Validation rules with executor URIs

  • Enums: Type definitions (LogicalType, Severity, etc.)

Schema¶

Schemas are first-class entities representing data assets.

from griot_core.models import Schema, Property
from griot_core.models.enums import SchemaStatus, LogicalType

schema = Schema(
    id="users-schema",
    name="Users",
    version="1.0.0",
    status=SchemaStatus.ACTIVE,
    physical_name="dim_users",
    description="User dimension table",
    owner_team="data-engineering",
    properties=[
        Property(
            id="user-id",
            name="user_id",
            logical_type=LogicalType.STRING,
            required=True,
            primary_key=True,
        ),
        Property(
            id="email",
            name="email",
            logical_type=LogicalType.STRING,
            is_pii=True,
        ),
    ],
)

Contract¶

Contracts define data quality expectations and can extend other contracts.

from griot_core.models import Contract
from griot_core.models.enums import ContractStatus

contract = Contract(
    id="users-contract",
    name="Users Contract",
    version="1.0.0",
    status=ContractStatus.ACTIVE,
    owner="data-engineering",
    extends="griot://templates/pii-contract@1.0",
    inline_schemas=[schema],
    checks=[...],
)

Property¶

Properties define columns/fields in a schema.

from griot_core.models import Property, PropertyConstraints
from griot_core.models.enums import LogicalType, PIIType

property = Property(
    id="email-property",
    name="email",
    logical_type=LogicalType.STRING,
    physical_type="VARCHAR(255)",
    description="User email address",
    required=True,
    unique=True,
    is_pii=True,
    pii_type=PIIType.EMAIL,
)

Check¶

Checks define validation rules with executor URIs.

from griot_core.models import Check, CheckCondition
from griot_core.models.enums import CheckType, Severity

check = Check(
    name="null_check",
    description="Ensure no nulls in required field",
    type=CheckType.DATA_QUALITY,
    executor="registry://executors/null-check@1.0",
    parameters={"column": "user_id", "threshold": 0},
    severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
    when=CheckCondition(
        environment=["production"],
        profile=["data_engineering"],
    ),
)

Enums¶

LogicalType¶

class LogicalType(str, Enum):
    STRING = "string"
    INTEGER = "integer"
    DECIMAL = "decimal"
    BOOLEAN = "boolean"
    DATE = "date"
    TIMESTAMP = "timestamp"
    ARRAY = "array"
    OBJECT = "object"

Severity¶

class Severity(str, Enum):
    CRITICAL = "critical"
    WARNING = "warning"
    INFO = "info"

CheckType¶

class CheckType(str, Enum):
    DATA_QUALITY = "data_quality"
    PRIVACY = "privacy"
    SCHEMA = "schema"

PIIType¶

class PIIType(str, Enum):
    NAME = "name"
    EMAIL = "email"
    PHONE = "phone"
    SSN = "ssn"
    NATIONAL_ID = "national_id"
    CREDIT_CARD = "credit_card"
    ...

API Reference¶

Griot Core Entity Models.

This module provides dataclasses for all core entities in the Griot data contract system, including Schema, Contract, Property, and Check.

class griot_core.models.Cardinality(*values)[source]¶

Cardinality of relationships.

ONE_TO_ONE = 'one_to_one'¶
ONE_TO_MANY = 'one_to_many'¶
MANY_TO_ONE = 'many_to_one'¶
MANY_TO_MANY = 'many_to_many'¶
class griot_core.models.CheckType(*values)[source]¶

Types of validation checks.

DATA_QUALITY = 'data_quality'¶
PRIVACY = 'privacy'¶
SCHEMA = 'schema'¶
class griot_core.models.ComplianceFramework(*values)[source]¶

Regulatory compliance frameworks.

GDPR = 'gdpr'¶
CCPA = 'ccpa'¶
HIPAA = 'hipaa'¶
PCI_DSS = 'pci_dss'¶
KENYA_DPA = 'kenya_dpa'¶
SOX = 'sox'¶
class griot_core.models.ContractStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a data contract in its lifecycle.

DRAFT = 'draft'¶
ACTIVE = 'active'¶
DEPRECATED = 'deprecated'¶
ARCHIVED = 'archived'¶
class griot_core.models.LogicalType(*values)[source]¶

Logical data types for schema properties.

STRING = 'string'¶
INTEGER = 'integer'¶
DECIMAL = 'decimal'¶
BOOLEAN = 'boolean'¶
DATE = 'date'¶
TIMESTAMP = 'timestamp'¶
ARRAY = 'array'¶
OBJECT = 'object'¶
class griot_core.models.MaskingStrategy(*values)[source]¶

Strategies for masking sensitive data.

NONE = 'none'¶
REDACT = 'redact'¶
HASH = 'hash'¶
PARTIAL = 'partial'¶
ASTERISK = 'asterisk'¶
NULL = 'null'¶
TOKENIZE = 'tokenize'¶
class griot_core.models.PIIType(*values)[source]¶

Types of Personally Identifiable Information.

NAME = 'name'¶
EMAIL = 'email'¶
PHONE = 'phone'¶
SSN = 'ssn'¶
NATIONAL_ID = 'national_id'¶
TAX_ID = 'tax_id'¶
IBAN = 'iban'¶
CREDIT_CARD = 'credit_card'¶
ADDRESS = 'address'¶
FINANCIAL = 'financial'¶
HEALTH = 'health'¶
BIOMETRIC = 'biometric'¶
IP_ADDRESS = 'ip_address'¶
MAC_ADDRESS = 'mac_address'¶
DATE_OF_BIRTH = 'date_of_birth'¶
PASSPORT = 'passport'¶
class griot_core.models.RelationshipType(*values)[source]¶

Types of relationships between properties/tables.

FOREIGN_KEY = 'foreign_key'¶
REFERENCES = 'references'¶
class griot_core.models.Runtime(*values)[source]¶

Executor runtime types.

WASM = 'wasm'¶
CONTAINER = 'container'¶
class griot_core.models.SchemaStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a schema in its lifecycle.

DRAFT = 'draft'¶
PENDING_REVIEW = 'pending_review'¶
ACTIVE = 'active'¶
DEPRECATED = 'deprecated'¶
class griot_core.models.Severity(*values)[source]¶

Severity levels for checks and alerts.

CRITICAL = 'critical'¶
WARNING = 'warning'¶
INFO = 'info'¶
class griot_core.models.Check(name, description, type, executor, parameters=<factory>, severity=Severity.WARNING, when=None, tags=<factory>)[source]¶

A validation check definition.

Checks are validation rules that specify what to validate and how. The actual validation logic is encapsulated in executors (WASM modules or container images) that are referenced by URI.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • description (str)

  • type (CheckType)

  • executor (str)

  • parameters (Dict[str, Any])

  • severity (Severity)

  • when (CheckCondition | None)

  • tags (List[str])

name¶

Unique name for this check (e.g., “salary_range_valid”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of what this check validates

Type:

str

type¶

The category of check (data_quality, privacy, schema)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.CheckType

executor¶

URI pointing to the executor that runs this check Examples: - “registry://executors/null-check@1.0” - “file://path/to/executor.wasm” - “oci://ghcr.io/griot/checks/drift:1.0”

Type:

str

parameters¶

Key-value pairs passed to the executor

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

severity¶

How critical a failure of this check is

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Severity

when¶

Optional condition for when this check should run

Type:

griot_core.models.check.CheckCondition | None

tags¶

Tags for categorizing and filtering checks

Type:

List[str]

severity: Severity = 'warning'¶
when: CheckCondition | None = None¶
name: str¶
description: str¶
type: CheckType¶
executor: str¶
parameters: Dict[str, Any]¶
tags: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.CheckCondition(environment=None, profile=None, tags=None)[source]¶

Condition for when a check should run.

Used to conditionally execute checks based on environment, profile, or other runtime parameters.

Parameters:

  • environment (List[str] | None)

  • profile (List[str] | None)

  • tags (List[str] | None)

environment: List[str] | None = None¶
profile: List[str] | None = None¶
tags: List[str] | None = None¶
class griot_core.models.Contract(api_version='v1.0.0', kind='DataContract', id='', name='', version='1.0.0', status=ContractStatus.DRAFT, extends=None, schema_refs=<factory>, inline_schemas=<factory>, description=None, owner=None, data_product=None, tags=<factory>, authoritative_definitions=<factory>, executors=None, checks=<factory>, compliance=None, sla=None, governance=None, servers=<factory>, team=None, support=<factory>, created_at=None, updated_at=None)[source]¶

A data contract - an agreement about one or more schemas.

Contracts can extend other contracts or templates to inherit configuration, overriding only what differs for the specific use case.

Parameters:

  • api_version (str)

  • kind (str)

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • version (str)

  • status (ContractStatus)

  • extends (Optional[str])

  • schema_refs (List[SchemaRef])

  • inline_schemas (List['Schema'])

  • description (Optional[ContractDescription])

  • owner (Optional[str])

  • data_product (Optional[str])

  • tags (List[str])

  • authoritative_definitions (List[AuthoritativeDefinition])

  • executors (Optional[ExecutorConfig])

  • checks (List['Check'])

  • compliance (Optional[ComplianceConfig])

  • sla (Optional[SLAConfig])

  • governance (Optional[GovernanceConfig])

  • servers (List[Server])

  • team (Optional[TeamConfig])

  • support (List[SupportChannel])

  • created_at (Optional[datetime])

  • updated_at (Optional[datetime])

api_version¶

API schema version (e.g., “v1.0.0”)

Type:

str

kind¶

Always “DataContract”

Type:

str

id¶

Unique identifier for this contract

Type:

str

name¶

Human-readable name

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

status¶

Lifecycle status (draft, active, deprecated, archived)

Type:

ContractStatus

extends¶

Optional URI to parent contract/template for inheritance

Type:

Optional[str]

schema_refs¶

References to standalone schemas

Type:

List[SchemaRef]

inline_schemas¶

Schemas defined inline within this contract

Type:

List[‘Schema’]

description¶

Contract description metadata

Type:

Optional[ContractDescription]

owner¶

Owner team/person

Type:

Optional[str]

data_product¶

Associated data product

Type:

Optional[str]

tags¶

Tags for categorization

Type:

List[str]

authoritative_definitions¶

Links to canonical sources

Type:

List[AuthoritativeDefinition]

executors¶

Executor configuration with profiles

Type:

Optional[ExecutorConfig]

checks¶

Contract-level checks (apply to all schemas)

Type:

List[‘Check’]

compliance¶

Compliance configuration

Type:

Optional[ComplianceConfig]

sla¶

SLA configuration

Type:

Optional[SLAConfig]

governance¶

Governance configuration

Type:

Optional[GovernanceConfig]

servers¶

Data source definitions

Type:

List[Server]

team¶

Team configuration

Type:

Optional[TeamConfig]

support¶

Support channels

Type:

List[SupportChannel]

api_version: str = 'v1.0.0'¶
compliance: ComplianceConfig | None = None¶
created_at: datetime | None = None¶
data_product: str | None = None¶
description: ContractDescription | None = None¶
executors: ExecutorConfig | None = None¶
extends: str | None = None¶
governance: GovernanceConfig | None = None¶
id: str = ''¶
kind: str = 'DataContract'¶
name: str = ''¶
owner: str | None = None¶
sla: SLAConfig | None = None¶
status: ContractStatus = 'draft'¶
team: TeamConfig | None = None¶
updated_at: datetime | None = None¶
version: str = '1.0.0'¶
schema_refs: List[SchemaRef]¶
inline_schemas: List['Schema']¶
tags: List[str]¶
authoritative_definitions: List[AuthoritativeDefinition]¶
checks: List['Check']¶
servers: List[Server]¶
support: List[SupportChannel]¶
class griot_core.models.Property(id, name, logical_type, physical_type, description, ordinal_position=0, constraints=<factory>, relationships=<factory>)[source]¶

A property (column/field) in a schema.

Properties are the building blocks of schemas, representing individual data elements with their types, constraints, and relationships.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • logical_type (LogicalType)

  • physical_type (str)

  • description (str)

  • ordinal_position (int)

  • constraints (PropertyConstraints)

  • relationships (List[Relationship])

id¶

Unique identifier for this property (e.g., “COL-001”)

Type:

str

name¶

The property name (e.g., “employee_id”)

Type:

str

logical_type¶

The logical data type (string, integer, etc.)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.LogicalType

physical_type¶

The physical type in the database (e.g., “VARCHAR(36)”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of this property

Type:

str

ordinal_position¶

Position of this column in the schema (0-indexed)

Type:

int

constraints¶

Data integrity, partitioning, and privacy constraints

Type:

griot_core.models.property.PropertyConstraints

relationships¶

Relationships to other properties/tables

Type:

List[griot_core.models.property.Relationship]

ordinal_position: int = 0¶
id: str¶
name: str¶
logical_type: LogicalType¶
physical_type: str¶
description: str¶
constraints: PropertyConstraints¶
relationships: List[Relationship]¶
class griot_core.models.PropertyConstraints(primary_key=False, required=False, unique=False, nullable=True, partitioned=False, partition_key_position=None, is_pii=False, pii_type=None)[source]¶

Unified constraints for a property.

Covers data integrity, partitioning, and privacy constraints.

Parameters:

  • primary_key (bool)

  • required (bool)

  • unique (bool)

  • nullable (bool)

  • partitioned (bool)

  • partition_key_position (int | None)

  • is_pii (bool)

  • pii_type (PIIType | None)

is_pii: bool = False¶
nullable: bool = True¶
partition_key_position: int | None = None¶
partitioned: bool = False¶
pii_type: PIIType | None = None¶
primary_key: bool = False¶
required: bool = False¶
unique: bool = False¶
class griot_core.models.Relationship(to, type, cardinality, description='')[source]¶

Defines a relationship from this property to another property/table.

Used for foreign keys and other references between schemas.

Parameters:

  • to (str)

  • type (RelationshipType)

  • cardinality (Cardinality)

  • description (str)

description: str = ''¶
to: str¶
type: RelationshipType¶
cardinality: Cardinality¶
class griot_core.models.Schema(id, name, version, status, physical_name, description, owner_team, properties=<factory>, checks=<factory>, tags=<factory>, created_at=None, updated_at=None)[source]¶

A standalone schema representing a data asset (table, view, file).

Schemas are first-class citizens in Griot. They exist independently of contracts and can be referenced by multiple contracts. This enables: - One schema definition, multiple contracts referencing it - Schema versioning independent of contract versioning - A browsable schema catalog - Clear ownership: Data Engineers own schemas, Governance owns contracts

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • version (str)

  • status (SchemaStatus)

  • physical_name (str)

  • description (str)

  • owner_team (str)

  • properties (List['Property'])

  • checks (List['Check'])

  • tags (List[str])

  • created_at (Optional[datetime])

  • updated_at (Optional[datetime])

id¶

Unique identifier for this schema (e.g., “sch-employees-001”)

Type:

str

name¶

Human-readable name (e.g., “employees”)

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

status¶

Lifecycle status (draft, pending_review, active, deprecated)

Type:

SchemaStatus

physical_name¶

The physical name in the data platform (e.g., “hr.employees_tbl”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of this schema

Type:

str

owner_team¶

The team responsible for this schema (e.g., “data-platform-team”)

Type:

str

properties¶

List of properties (columns) in this schema

Type:

List[‘Property’]

checks¶

Optional schema-level validation checks

Type:

List[‘Check’]

tags¶

Tags for categorization and discovery

Type:

List[str]

created_at¶

When this schema was created

Type:

Optional[datetime]

updated_at¶

When this schema was last modified

Type:

Optional[datetime]

created_at: datetime | None = None¶
updated_at: datetime | None = None¶
id: str¶
name: str¶
version: str¶
status: SchemaStatus¶
physical_name: str¶
description: str¶
owner_team: str¶
properties: List['Property']¶
checks: List['Check']¶
tags: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.SchemaRef(ref, version_pinned=True, additional_checks=<factory>)[source]¶

Reference to a standalone schema.

Used when a contract references an external schema instead of defining it inline.

Parameters:

  • ref (str)

  • version_pinned (bool)

  • additional_checks (List['Check'])

ref¶

URI to the schema (e.g., “registry://schemas/employees@1.0.0”)

Type:

str

version_pinned¶

Whether the version is pinned or can float

Type:

bool

additional_checks¶

Extra checks to apply beyond the schema’s own checks

Type:

List[‘Check’]

version_pinned: bool = True¶
ref: str¶
additional_checks: List['Check']¶
class griot_core.models.ContractDescription(purpose='', usage='', limitations='')[source]¶

Contract description metadata.

Provides human-readable context about the contract’s purpose and usage.

Parameters:

  • purpose (str)

  • usage (str)

  • limitations (str)

limitations: str = ''¶
purpose: str = ''¶
usage: str = ''¶
class griot_core.models.ExecutorConfig(default_runtime_preference=<factory>, default_timeout='300s', registry='registry://executors', auto_checks=<factory>, profiles=<factory>)[source]¶

Executor configuration for the contract.

Defines default runtime settings, auto-check configuration, and profile-specific overrides.

Parameters:

  • default_runtime_preference (List[str])

  • default_timeout (str)

  • registry (str)

  • auto_checks (AutoCheckConfig)

  • profiles (Dict[str, ExecutorProfile])

default_timeout: str = '300s'¶
registry: str = 'registry://executors'¶
default_runtime_preference: List[str]¶
auto_checks: AutoCheckConfig¶
profiles: Dict[str, ExecutorProfile]¶
class griot_core.models.ExecutorProfile(description='', checks_include=<factory>, checks_exclude=<factory>, runtime_preference=<factory>, custom_executors=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a specific execution profile.

Different teams (data engineering, software engineering, etc.) can have different check configurations and runtime preferences.

Parameters:

  • description (str)

  • checks_include (List[str])

  • checks_exclude (List[str])

  • runtime_preference (List[str])

  • custom_executors (List[Dict[str, Any]])

description: str = ''¶
checks_include: List[str]¶
checks_exclude: List[str]¶
runtime_preference: List[str]¶
custom_executors: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
class griot_core.models.AutoCheckConfig(enabled=True, include=<factory>, severity=Severity.CRITICAL)[source]¶

Configuration for auto-generated checks from constraints.

Auto-checks are automatically created based on property constraints (nullable, unique, primary_key, required, pii_masking).

Parameters:

  • enabled (bool)

  • include (List[str])

  • severity (Severity)

enabled: bool = True¶
severity: Severity = 'critical'¶
include: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.ComplianceConfig(classification='internal', legal=<factory>, data_subject_rights=<factory>, retention=<factory>, cross_border=<factory>, audit=<factory>, export_controls=<factory>)[source]¶

Compliance configuration for the contract.

Covers data classification, legal requirements, data subject rights, retention, cross-border transfers, audit, and export controls.

Parameters:

  • classification (str)

  • legal (LegalConfig)

  • data_subject_rights (DataSubjectRights)

  • retention (RetentionPolicy)

  • cross_border (CrossBorderConfig)

  • audit (AuditConfig)

  • export_controls (ExportControls)

classification: str = 'internal'¶
legal: LegalConfig¶
data_subject_rights: DataSubjectRights¶
retention: RetentionPolicy¶
cross_border: CrossBorderConfig¶
audit: AuditConfig¶
export_controls: ExportControls¶
class griot_core.models.LegalConfig(jurisdiction=<factory>, legal_basis='legitimate_interest', data_controller='', data_processor=None, regulations=<factory>)[source]¶

Legal and regulatory configuration.

Parameters:

  • jurisdiction (List[str])

  • legal_basis (str)

  • data_controller (str)

  • data_processor (str | None)

  • regulations (List[Regulation])

data_controller: str = ''¶
data_processor: str | None = None¶
legal_basis: str = 'legitimate_interest'¶
jurisdiction: List[str]¶
regulations: List[Regulation]¶
class griot_core.models.Regulation(name, applicable=True, articles=<factory>, documentation='')[source]¶

Regulatory compliance configuration.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • applicable (bool)

  • articles (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • documentation (str)

applicable: bool = True¶
documentation: str = ''¶
name: str¶
articles: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
class griot_core.models.DataSubjectRights(access_request=<factory>, erasure_request=<factory>, portability_request=<factory>)[source]¶

Collection of data subject rights configurations.

Parameters:

  • access_request (DataSubjectRight)

  • erasure_request (DataSubjectRight)

  • portability_request (DataSubjectRight)

access_request: DataSubjectRight¶
erasure_request: DataSubjectRight¶
portability_request: DataSubjectRight¶
class griot_core.models.DataSubjectRight(enabled=False, sla='P30D', endpoint='', exceptions=<factory>, formats=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a data subject right (GDPR/CCPA).

Parameters:

  • enabled (bool)

  • sla (str)

  • endpoint (str)

  • exceptions (List[str])

  • formats (List[str])

endpoint: str = ''¶
sla: str = 'P30D'¶
exceptions: List[str]¶
formats: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.RetentionPolicy(period='P7Y', policy='archive', exceptions=<factory>, review_schedule='P1Y')[source]¶

Data retention policy configuration.

Parameters:

  • period (str)

  • policy (str)

  • exceptions (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • review_schedule (str)

period: str = 'P7Y'¶
policy: str = 'archive'¶
review_schedule: str = 'P1Y'¶
exceptions: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
class griot_core.models.CrossBorderConfig(transfers_allowed=False, approved_countries=<factory>, transfer_mechanisms=<factory>, restrictions=<factory>)[source]¶

Cross-border data transfer configuration.

Parameters:

  • transfers_allowed (bool)

  • approved_countries (List[str])

  • transfer_mechanisms (Dict[str, bool])

  • restrictions (List[str])

transfers_allowed: bool = False¶
approved_countries: List[str]¶
transfer_mechanisms: Dict[str, bool]¶
restrictions: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.AuditConfig(logging_required=True, log_retention='P365D', logged_events=<factory>, audit_log_location='')[source]¶

Audit logging configuration.

Parameters:

  • logging_required (bool)

  • log_retention (str)

  • logged_events (List[str])

  • audit_log_location (str)

audit_log_location: str = ''¶
log_retention: str = 'P365D'¶
logging_required: bool = True¶
logged_events: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.ExportControls(bulk_download_allowed=False, api_access_allowed=True, rate_limit='1000/hour', requires_approval=<factory>)[source]¶

Data export controls configuration.

Parameters:

  • bulk_download_allowed (bool)

  • api_access_allowed (bool)

  • rate_limit (str)

  • requires_approval (List[str])

api_access_allowed: bool = True¶
bulk_download_allowed: bool = False¶
rate_limit: str = '1000/hour'¶
requires_approval: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.SLAConfig(freshness_max_age='24h', freshness_column='', availability_schedule='00:00-23:59 UTC', availability_target_uptime='99%', volume_expected_daily_records=None, volume_tolerance_percent=20, volume_alert_on_anomaly=True, quality_minimum_completeness='99%', quality_maximum_null_rate='1%')[source]¶

SLA configuration for the contract.

Defines freshness, availability, volume, and quality targets.

Parameters:

  • freshness_max_age (str)

  • freshness_column (str)

  • availability_schedule (str)

  • availability_target_uptime (str)

  • volume_expected_daily_records (int | None)

  • volume_tolerance_percent (int)

  • volume_alert_on_anomaly (bool)

  • quality_minimum_completeness (str)

  • quality_maximum_null_rate (str)

availability_schedule: str = '00:00-23:59 UTC'¶
availability_target_uptime: str = '99%'¶
freshness_column: str = ''¶
freshness_max_age: str = '24h'¶
quality_maximum_null_rate: str = '1%'¶
quality_minimum_completeness: str = '99%'¶
volume_alert_on_anomaly: bool = True¶
volume_expected_daily_records: int | None = None¶
volume_tolerance_percent: int = 20¶
class griot_core.models.GovernanceConfig(review=<factory>, change_management=<factory>, approval_workflow=<factory>)[source]¶

Governance configuration for the contract.

Covers review schedules, change management, and approval workflows.

Parameters:

  • review (ReviewConfig)

  • change_management (ChangeManagement)

  • approval_workflow (ApprovalWorkflow)

review: ReviewConfig¶
change_management: ChangeManagement¶
approval_workflow: ApprovalWorkflow¶
class griot_core.models.ReviewConfig(cadence='quarterly', last_review=None, next_review=None, reviewers=<factory>)[source]¶

Governance review configuration.

Parameters:

  • cadence (str)

  • last_review (datetime | None)

  • next_review (datetime | None)

  • reviewers (List[str])

cadence: str = 'quarterly'¶
last_review: datetime | None = None¶
next_review: datetime | None = None¶
reviewers: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.ChangeManagement(breaking_change_notice='P30D', deprecation_notice='P90D', migration_support=True)[source]¶

Change management configuration.

Parameters:

  • breaking_change_notice (str)

  • deprecation_notice (str)

  • migration_support (bool)

breaking_change_notice: str = 'P30D'¶
deprecation_notice: str = 'P90D'¶
migration_support: bool = True¶
class griot_core.models.ApprovalWorkflow(schema_changes=<factory>, check_changes=<factory>, compliance_changes=<factory>)[source]¶

Approval workflow for different change types.

Parameters:

  • schema_changes (List[str])

  • check_changes (List[str])

  • compliance_changes (List[str])

schema_changes: List[str]¶
check_changes: List[str]¶
compliance_changes: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.Server(name, type, environment, connection=<factory>, roles=<factory>)[source]¶

Server/data source definition.

Defines where the data lives and how to connect to it.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • type (str)

  • environment (str)

  • connection (Dict[str, Any])

  • roles (List[str])

name: str¶
type: str¶
environment: str¶
connection: Dict[str, Any]¶
roles: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.TeamConfig(name='', description='', members=<factory>)[source]¶

Team configuration for the contract.

Defines who is responsible for the contract.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • description (str)

  • members (List[str])

description: str = ''¶
name: str = ''¶
members: List[str]¶
class griot_core.models.TeamMember(email, role='member')[source]¶

A team member.

Parameters:

  • email (str)

  • role (str)

role: str = 'member'¶
email: str¶
class griot_core.models.SupportChannel(type, contact)[source]¶

Support channel definition.

Defines how to get help with this contract.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • contact (str)

type: str¶
contact: str¶
class griot_core.models.AuthoritativeDefinition(type, url, description='')[source]¶

Reference to an authoritative definition.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • url (str)

  • description (str)

description: str = ''¶
type: str¶
url: str¶

Enums for the Griot data contract system.

This module defines all enumeration types used throughout griot-core, including statuses, types, severities, and compliance frameworks.

class griot_core.models.enums.SchemaStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a schema in its lifecycle.

class griot_core.models.enums.ContractStatus(*values)[source]¶

Status of a data contract in its lifecycle.

class griot_core.models.enums.LogicalType(*values)[source]¶

Logical data types for schema properties.

class griot_core.models.enums.PIIType(*values)[source]¶

Types of Personally Identifiable Information.

class griot_core.models.enums.CheckType(*values)[source]¶

Types of validation checks.

class griot_core.models.enums.Severity(*values)[source]¶

Severity levels for checks and alerts.

class griot_core.models.enums.Runtime(*values)[source]¶

Executor runtime types.

class griot_core.models.enums.RelationshipType(*values)[source]¶

Types of relationships between properties/tables.

class griot_core.models.enums.Cardinality(*values)[source]¶

Cardinality of relationships.

class griot_core.models.enums.MaskingStrategy(*values)[source]¶

Strategies for masking sensitive data.

class griot_core.models.enums.ComplianceFramework(*values)[source]¶

Regulatory compliance frameworks.

Schema model - First-class entity representing a data asset.

Schemas represent data assets (tables, views, files) and exist independently of contracts. Multiple contracts can reference the same schema.

class griot_core.models.schema.Schema(id, name, version, status, physical_name, description, owner_team, properties=<factory>, checks=<factory>, tags=<factory>, created_at=None, updated_at=None)[source]¶

A standalone schema representing a data asset (table, view, file).

Schemas are first-class citizens in Griot. They exist independently of contracts and can be referenced by multiple contracts. This enables: - One schema definition, multiple contracts referencing it - Schema versioning independent of contract versioning - A browsable schema catalog - Clear ownership: Data Engineers own schemas, Governance owns contracts

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • version (str)

  • status (SchemaStatus)

  • physical_name (str)

  • description (str)

  • owner_team (str)

  • properties (List['Property'])

  • checks (List['Check'])

  • tags (List[str])

  • created_at (Optional[datetime])

  • updated_at (Optional[datetime])

id¶

Unique identifier for this schema (e.g., “sch-employees-001”)

Type:

str

name¶

Human-readable name (e.g., “employees”)

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

status¶

Lifecycle status (draft, pending_review, active, deprecated)

Type:

SchemaStatus

physical_name¶

The physical name in the data platform (e.g., “hr.employees_tbl”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of this schema

Type:

str

owner_team¶

The team responsible for this schema (e.g., “data-platform-team”)

Type:

str

properties¶

List of properties (columns) in this schema

Type:

List[‘Property’]

checks¶

Optional schema-level validation checks

Type:

List[‘Check’]

tags¶

Tags for categorization and discovery

Type:

List[str]

created_at¶

When this schema was created

Type:

Optional[datetime]

updated_at¶

When this schema was last modified

Type:

Optional[datetime]

Contract model with inheritance support.

Contracts are agreements about data assets (schemas). They can extend other contracts or templates to inherit configuration, overriding only what differs for the specific use case.

class griot_core.models.contract.SchemaRef(ref, version_pinned=True, additional_checks=<factory>)[source]¶

Reference to a standalone schema.

Used when a contract references an external schema instead of defining it inline.

Parameters:

  • ref (str)

  • version_pinned (bool)

  • additional_checks (List['Check'])

ref¶

URI to the schema (e.g., “registry://schemas/employees@1.0.0”)

Type:

str

version_pinned¶

Whether the version is pinned or can float

Type:

bool

additional_checks¶

Extra checks to apply beyond the schema’s own checks

Type:

List[‘Check’]

class griot_core.models.contract.ContractDescription(purpose='', usage='', limitations='')[source]¶

Contract description metadata.

Provides human-readable context about the contract’s purpose and usage.

Parameters:

  • purpose (str)

  • usage (str)

  • limitations (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.ExecutorProfile(description='', checks_include=<factory>, checks_exclude=<factory>, runtime_preference=<factory>, custom_executors=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a specific execution profile.

Different teams (data engineering, software engineering, etc.) can have different check configurations and runtime preferences.

Parameters:

  • description (str)

  • checks_include (List[str])

  • checks_exclude (List[str])

  • runtime_preference (List[str])

  • custom_executors (List[Dict[str, Any]])

class griot_core.models.contract.AutoCheckConfig(enabled=True, include=<factory>, severity=Severity.CRITICAL)[source]¶

Configuration for auto-generated checks from constraints.

Auto-checks are automatically created based on property constraints (nullable, unique, primary_key, required, pii_masking).

Parameters:

  • enabled (bool)

  • include (List[str])

  • severity (Severity)

class griot_core.models.contract.ExecutorConfig(default_runtime_preference=<factory>, default_timeout='300s', registry='registry://executors', auto_checks=<factory>, profiles=<factory>)[source]¶

Executor configuration for the contract.

Defines default runtime settings, auto-check configuration, and profile-specific overrides.

Parameters:

  • default_runtime_preference (List[str])

  • default_timeout (str)

  • registry (str)

  • auto_checks (AutoCheckConfig)

  • profiles (Dict[str, ExecutorProfile])

class griot_core.models.contract.DataSubjectRight(enabled=False, sla='P30D', endpoint='', exceptions=<factory>, formats=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a data subject right (GDPR/CCPA).

Parameters:

  • enabled (bool)

  • sla (str)

  • endpoint (str)

  • exceptions (List[str])

  • formats (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.DataSubjectRights(access_request=<factory>, erasure_request=<factory>, portability_request=<factory>)[source]¶

Collection of data subject rights configurations.

Parameters:

  • access_request (DataSubjectRight)

  • erasure_request (DataSubjectRight)

  • portability_request (DataSubjectRight)

class griot_core.models.contract.RetentionPolicy(period='P7Y', policy='archive', exceptions=<factory>, review_schedule='P1Y')[source]¶

Data retention policy configuration.

Parameters:

  • period (str)

  • policy (str)

  • exceptions (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • review_schedule (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.CrossBorderConfig(transfers_allowed=False, approved_countries=<factory>, transfer_mechanisms=<factory>, restrictions=<factory>)[source]¶

Cross-border data transfer configuration.

Parameters:

  • transfers_allowed (bool)

  • approved_countries (List[str])

  • transfer_mechanisms (Dict[str, bool])

  • restrictions (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.AuditConfig(logging_required=True, log_retention='P365D', logged_events=<factory>, audit_log_location='')[source]¶

Audit logging configuration.

Parameters:

  • logging_required (bool)

  • log_retention (str)

  • logged_events (List[str])

  • audit_log_location (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.ExportControls(bulk_download_allowed=False, api_access_allowed=True, rate_limit='1000/hour', requires_approval=<factory>)[source]¶

Data export controls configuration.

Parameters:

  • bulk_download_allowed (bool)

  • api_access_allowed (bool)

  • rate_limit (str)

  • requires_approval (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.Regulation(name, applicable=True, articles=<factory>, documentation='')[source]¶

Regulatory compliance configuration.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • applicable (bool)

  • articles (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • documentation (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.LegalConfig(jurisdiction=<factory>, legal_basis='legitimate_interest', data_controller='', data_processor=None, regulations=<factory>)[source]¶

Legal and regulatory configuration.

Parameters:

  • jurisdiction (List[str])

  • legal_basis (str)

  • data_controller (str)

  • data_processor (str | None)

  • regulations (List[Regulation])

class griot_core.models.contract.ComplianceConfig(classification='internal', legal=<factory>, data_subject_rights=<factory>, retention=<factory>, cross_border=<factory>, audit=<factory>, export_controls=<factory>)[source]¶

Compliance configuration for the contract.

Covers data classification, legal requirements, data subject rights, retention, cross-border transfers, audit, and export controls.

Parameters:

  • classification (str)

  • legal (LegalConfig)

  • data_subject_rights (DataSubjectRights)

  • retention (RetentionPolicy)

  • cross_border (CrossBorderConfig)

  • audit (AuditConfig)

  • export_controls (ExportControls)

class griot_core.models.contract.SLAConfig(freshness_max_age='24h', freshness_column='', availability_schedule='00:00-23:59 UTC', availability_target_uptime='99%', volume_expected_daily_records=None, volume_tolerance_percent=20, volume_alert_on_anomaly=True, quality_minimum_completeness='99%', quality_maximum_null_rate='1%')[source]¶

SLA configuration for the contract.

Defines freshness, availability, volume, and quality targets.

Parameters:

  • freshness_max_age (str)

  • freshness_column (str)

  • availability_schedule (str)

  • availability_target_uptime (str)

  • volume_expected_daily_records (int | None)

  • volume_tolerance_percent (int)

  • volume_alert_on_anomaly (bool)

  • quality_minimum_completeness (str)

  • quality_maximum_null_rate (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.ReviewConfig(cadence='quarterly', last_review=None, next_review=None, reviewers=<factory>)[source]¶

Governance review configuration.

Parameters:

  • cadence (str)

  • last_review (datetime | None)

  • next_review (datetime | None)

  • reviewers (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.ChangeManagement(breaking_change_notice='P30D', deprecation_notice='P90D', migration_support=True)[source]¶

Change management configuration.

Parameters:

  • breaking_change_notice (str)

  • deprecation_notice (str)

  • migration_support (bool)

class griot_core.models.contract.ApprovalWorkflow(schema_changes=<factory>, check_changes=<factory>, compliance_changes=<factory>)[source]¶

Approval workflow for different change types.

Parameters:

  • schema_changes (List[str])

  • check_changes (List[str])

  • compliance_changes (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.GovernanceConfig(review=<factory>, change_management=<factory>, approval_workflow=<factory>)[source]¶

Governance configuration for the contract.

Covers review schedules, change management, and approval workflows.

Parameters:

  • review (ReviewConfig)

  • change_management (ChangeManagement)

  • approval_workflow (ApprovalWorkflow)

class griot_core.models.contract.Server(name, type, environment, connection=<factory>, roles=<factory>)[source]¶

Server/data source definition.

Defines where the data lives and how to connect to it.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • type (str)

  • environment (str)

  • connection (Dict[str, Any])

  • roles (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.TeamMember(email, role='member')[source]¶

A team member.

Parameters:

  • email (str)

  • role (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.TeamConfig(name='', description='', members=<factory>)[source]¶

Team configuration for the contract.

Defines who is responsible for the contract.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • description (str)

  • members (List[str])

class griot_core.models.contract.SupportChannel(type, contact)[source]¶

Support channel definition.

Defines how to get help with this contract.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • contact (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.AuthoritativeDefinition(type, url, description='')[source]¶

Reference to an authoritative definition.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • url (str)

  • description (str)

class griot_core.models.contract.Contract(api_version='v1.0.0', kind='DataContract', id='', name='', version='1.0.0', status=ContractStatus.DRAFT, extends=None, schema_refs=<factory>, inline_schemas=<factory>, description=None, owner=None, data_product=None, tags=<factory>, authoritative_definitions=<factory>, executors=None, checks=<factory>, compliance=None, sla=None, governance=None, servers=<factory>, team=None, support=<factory>, created_at=None, updated_at=None)[source]¶

A data contract - an agreement about one or more schemas.

Contracts can extend other contracts or templates to inherit configuration, overriding only what differs for the specific use case.

Parameters:

  • api_version (str)

  • kind (str)

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • version (str)

  • status (ContractStatus)

  • extends (Optional[str])

  • schema_refs (List[SchemaRef])

  • inline_schemas (List['Schema'])

  • description (Optional[ContractDescription])

  • owner (Optional[str])

  • data_product (Optional[str])

  • tags (List[str])

  • authoritative_definitions (List[AuthoritativeDefinition])

  • executors (Optional[ExecutorConfig])

  • checks (List['Check'])

  • compliance (Optional[ComplianceConfig])

  • sla (Optional[SLAConfig])

  • governance (Optional[GovernanceConfig])

  • servers (List[Server])

  • team (Optional[TeamConfig])

  • support (List[SupportChannel])

  • created_at (Optional[datetime])

  • updated_at (Optional[datetime])

api_version¶

API schema version (e.g., “v1.0.0”)

Type:

str

kind¶

Always “DataContract”

Type:

str

id¶

Unique identifier for this contract

Type:

str

name¶

Human-readable name

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

status¶

Lifecycle status (draft, active, deprecated, archived)

Type:

ContractStatus

extends¶

Optional URI to parent contract/template for inheritance

Type:

Optional[str]

schema_refs¶

References to standalone schemas

Type:

List[SchemaRef]

inline_schemas¶

Schemas defined inline within this contract

Type:

List[‘Schema’]

description¶

Contract description metadata

Type:

Optional[ContractDescription]

owner¶

Owner team/person

Type:

Optional[str]

data_product¶

Associated data product

Type:

Optional[str]

tags¶

Tags for categorization

Type:

List[str]

authoritative_definitions¶

Links to canonical sources

Type:

List[AuthoritativeDefinition]

executors¶

Executor configuration with profiles

Type:

Optional[ExecutorConfig]

checks¶

Contract-level checks (apply to all schemas)

Type:

List[‘Check’]

compliance¶

Compliance configuration

Type:

Optional[ComplianceConfig]

sla¶

SLA configuration

Type:

Optional[SLAConfig]

governance¶

Governance configuration

Type:

Optional[GovernanceConfig]

servers¶

Data source definitions

Type:

List[Server]

team¶

Team configuration

Type:

Optional[TeamConfig]

support¶

Support channels

Type:

List[SupportChannel]

Property model for schema columns/fields.

Properties represent individual columns or fields in a schema, with unified constraints covering data integrity, partitioning, and privacy.

class griot_core.models.property.PropertyConstraints(primary_key=False, required=False, unique=False, nullable=True, partitioned=False, partition_key_position=None, is_pii=False, pii_type=None)[source]¶

Unified constraints for a property.

Covers data integrity, partitioning, and privacy constraints.

Parameters:

  • primary_key (bool)

  • required (bool)

  • unique (bool)

  • nullable (bool)

  • partitioned (bool)

  • partition_key_position (int | None)

  • is_pii (bool)

  • pii_type (PIIType | None)

class griot_core.models.property.Relationship(to, type, cardinality, description='')[source]¶

Defines a relationship from this property to another property/table.

Used for foreign keys and other references between schemas.

Parameters:

  • to (str)

  • type (RelationshipType)

  • cardinality (Cardinality)

  • description (str)

class griot_core.models.property.Property(id, name, logical_type, physical_type, description, ordinal_position=0, constraints=<factory>, relationships=<factory>)[source]¶

A property (column/field) in a schema.

Properties are the building blocks of schemas, representing individual data elements with their types, constraints, and relationships.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • name (str)

  • logical_type (LogicalType)

  • physical_type (str)

  • description (str)

  • ordinal_position (int)

  • constraints (PropertyConstraints)

  • relationships (List[Relationship])

id¶

Unique identifier for this property (e.g., “COL-001”)

Type:

str

name¶

The property name (e.g., “employee_id”)

Type:

str

logical_type¶

The logical data type (string, integer, etc.)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.LogicalType

physical_type¶

The physical type in the database (e.g., “VARCHAR(36)”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of this property

Type:

str

ordinal_position¶

Position of this column in the schema (0-indexed)

Type:

int

constraints¶

Data integrity, partitioning, and privacy constraints

Type:

griot_core.models.property.PropertyConstraints

relationships¶

Relationships to other properties/tables

Type:

List[griot_core.models.property.Relationship]

Check model for validation definitions.

Checks define validation rules that are executed against data. Each check specifies an executor URI that points to a WASM module or container image that performs the actual validation.

class griot_core.models.check.CheckCondition(environment=None, profile=None, tags=None)[source]¶

Condition for when a check should run.

Used to conditionally execute checks based on environment, profile, or other runtime parameters.

Parameters:

  • environment (List[str] | None)

  • profile (List[str] | None)

  • tags (List[str] | None)

class griot_core.models.check.Check(name, description, type, executor, parameters=<factory>, severity=Severity.WARNING, when=None, tags=<factory>)[source]¶

A validation check definition.

Checks are validation rules that specify what to validate and how. The actual validation logic is encapsulated in executors (WASM modules or container images) that are referenced by URI.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • description (str)

  • type (CheckType)

  • executor (str)

  • parameters (Dict[str, Any])

  • severity (Severity)

  • when (CheckCondition | None)

  • tags (List[str])

name¶

Unique name for this check (e.g., “salary_range_valid”)

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description of what this check validates

Type:

str

type¶

The category of check (data_quality, privacy, schema)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.CheckType

executor¶

URI pointing to the executor that runs this check Examples: - “registry://executors/null-check@1.0” - “file://path/to/executor.wasm” - “oci://ghcr.io/griot/checks/drift:1.0”

Type:

str

parameters¶

Key-value pairs passed to the executor

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

severity¶

How critical a failure of this check is

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Severity

when¶

Optional condition for when this check should run

Type:

griot_core.models.check.CheckCondition | None

tags¶

Tags for categorizing and filtering checks

Type:

List[str]

Next
Parsing
Previous
